↩️ Reply to OSINT Status-6 (War & Military News) It is believed that the notorious pro-Kremlin Russian milblogger and crew-member of Kamov Ka-52 attack helicopter, Alexey Zemtsov, known on Telegram as "Voevoda Broadcasts (Воевода Вещает)," has committed suicide. In a lengthy, 16-min video published on Telegram on Wednesday Zemtsov describes his past conflicts with superiors over his online activity. But the main plot is this: in October 2025, "Voevoda" becomes aware that his wife is cheating on him, so he decides to beat the guy whom she is sleeping with. For that he is taken to custody and later sent to assault units. During courses for platoon commanders in April 2026, "Voevoda" publishes a photo of an old, Soviet-made SSh-68 steel helmet that was distributed to a serviceman who was supposed to take part in combat operations. For that, "Voevoda" is accused of discreditation of the Russian army and threatened with return to custody. "I don't want to live with the shame, with discreditation of the Russian army," milblogger Alexey Zemtsov declared in his farewell video. tweet

OSINTtechnical A blaze tore through one of Australia's two remaining oil refineries overnight. The Corio Refinery produces 50% of Victoria's and 10% of Australia's fuel, with Australia’s energy minister adding that jet fuel and gasoline production would be disrupted. https://twitter.com/Osinttechnical/status/2044534830747009254/photo/1 tweet

↩️ Reply to WarFront Witness: "⚡️🇺🇸🇮🇱🇱🇧 Axios: U.S. officials say the U.S. haven't asked Israel for a ceasefire in Lebanon and it's not part of the peace negotiations with Iran. However, the President would welcome and be happy with an end of hostilities as part of an agreement between…" ⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇱 🇱🇧 Axios: The possibility of a ceasefire was discussed in a meeting between U.S. Secretary of State Rubio, Israeli Ambassador to the U.S. Leiter, and Lebanese Ambassador to the U.S. Hamadeh. A source says the proposal included confidence-building measures by both sides, with Israel expected to consider a ceasefire as part of the framework. The Israeli and Lebanese ambassadors said they would consult with Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu and Lebanese President Aoun before returning with responses. @wfwitness

Status-6 (War & Military News) RT @DanLamothe : Attempted evasion reported: Yesterday, an Iranian-flagged cargo vessel tried to evade the U.S. blockade after leaving Bandar Abbas, exiting the Strait of Hormuz, and transiting along the Iranian coastline. The guided-missile destroyer USS Spruance (DDG 111) successfully redirected the vessel, which is heading back to Iran. Ten vessels have now been turned around and ZERO ships have broken through since the start of the U.S. blockade on Monday. - U.S. Central Command tweet

OSINTdefender According to U.S. Central Command (CENTCOM), yesterday, an Iranian-flagged cargo vessel sailing from the Iranian port of Bandar Abbas attempted to evade the U.S. blockade of vessels going to and from Iranian ports through the Strait of Hormuz and the guided missile destroyer USS Spruance (DDG 111) redirected the vessel, causing it to head back to Iran. Per the release, 10 vessels have now been turned around since the blockade began. tweet
